zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Porter Jr於大約1330年出生在英國越可爾時，他的父親Edward de St John72歲，母親Eve Dawtrey47歲。他和Anastasia Aton至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1389年3月7日在他的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edward Porter Jr于大约1330年出生在英国越可爾时，他的父亲Edward de St John72岁，母亲Eve Dawtrey47岁。他和Anastasia Aton至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1389年3月7日在他的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Porter Jr was born about 1330, in Yorkshire, England, his father, Edward de St John, was 72 and his mother, Eve Dawtrey, was 47. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Anastasia Aton. He died on 7 March 1389, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward Porter Jr est né environ 1330, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Edward de St John, avait 72 ans et sa mère, Eve Dawtrey, avait 47 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Anastasia Aton. Il est décédé le 7 mars 1389, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edward Porter Jr um 1330 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward de St John 72 und seine Mutter Eve Dawtrey 47. Er hatte mit Anastasia Aton mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 7. März 1389 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Porter Jr è nato il circa 1330 nel Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Edward de St John, aveva 72 anni e il suo madre, Eve Dawtrey, aveva 47 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Anastasia Aton. Morì il 7 marzo 1389, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.Hampshire, England: イギリスハンプシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Porter Jrが約1330年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Edward de St Johnは72歳，母Eve Dawtreyは47歳であった。彼はAnastasia Atonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1389年3月7日に故郷で60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Porter Jr(이)가 대략 1330년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward de St John(은)는 72세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve Dawtrey(은)는 47세였다. 그는 Anastasia Aton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1389년 3월 7일 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Porter Jr nasceu aproximadamente 1330, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward de St John, tinha 72 anos e sua mãe, Eve Dawtrey, tinha 47 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Anastasia Aton. Ele faleceu em 7 de março de 1389, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Porter Jr родился около 1330 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward de St John было 72 года, а матери, которую звали Eve Dawtrey, 47. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Anastasia Aton. Он умер 7 марта 1389 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward Porter Jr nació aproximadamente 1330, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward de St John, tenía 72 años y su mamá, Eve Dawtrey, tenía 47 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Anastasia Aton. Murió el 7 de marzo de 1389, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años.
